primary_reason: This five-year evaluation measures completeness, timeliness, alert verification, and response functions of an emergency infectious-disease surveillance system in a conflict setting.
This letter develops and ROC-validates a weighted modification of NEWS2 for mortality and intervention risk stratification in emphysematous pyelonephritis.
This experimental and simulated epidemic study evaluates whether early-warning signals reliably predict climate-mediated epidemic emergence and identifies substantial false-positive risk.
This bioinformatics study develops biomarkers for cardio-renal syndrome rather than an infectious-disease outcome with a concrete transferable surveillance route.
This prospective household cohort estimates secondary attack rates, serial intervals, reproduction numbers, and index-case misclassification sensitivity for clade Ib mpox transmission.
This study prospectively validates county-level machine-learning models trained on Alpha outcomes to rank Omicron incidence risk.
This paper introduces and tests a sequence-composition anomaly-detection method for early identification of influenza A spillovers from few sequences.
This pilot demonstrates implementation of offline mobile early-warning and event-based surveillance for priority infectious diseases in conflict-affected Darfur.
This study develops an interpretable Isolation Forest framework that identifies anomalous environmental profiles as potential antimicrobial-resistance surveillance risks.
This retrospective analysis assesses spatiotemporal OSINT disease signals around floods as a surveillance input for infectious-disease preparedness.
This published erratum corrects a COVID-19 vaccination evaluation based on a counterfactual reproduction-number method.
This large-scale implementation evaluates self-collected saliva for integrated SARS-CoV-2 clinical surveillance, genomic tracking, and rapid lineage proxy assignment.
This framework concerns chemical ecotoxicity in aquatic organisms rather than infectious-disease surveillance or transmission.
This study estimates herd-level reproduction numbers for African swine fever with deterministic and stochastic models across national and regional production systems.
This paper develops general theoretical frameworks linking long-term susceptible fractions to reproduction numbers in networked epidemic models.
This application fits a colonization-transmission and observation model to compare reproduction numbers across pandemic Escherichia coli clones.
This retrospective cohort evaluates the prognostic performance of MEWS and TREWS for ICU admission and death in hospitalized COVID-19 patients.
This phylogenetic and sequence-surveillance analysis reconstructs H5N1 introductions and identifies backyard poultry as a potential early-warning sentinel.
This paper develops a six-step evidence-driven process for selecting pathogens and customizing multiplex panels for acute-febrile-illness surveillance.
This multi-pathogen surveillance study characterizes clinically indistinguishable coinfections among febrile patients using a customized molecular panel.
This application fits a Bayesian transmission model to mixed case and syndromic surveillance streams while incorporating time-varying behavioural responses.
This study develops and interprets a graph-based machine-learning and Bayesian-network framework for environmental AMR risk prediction in poultry production.
The study develops, benchmarks, and field-validates a spatial infectious-outbreak forecasting system.
This food-contaminant biosensor review concerns analytical product technology rather than infectious-disease epidemiological measurement.
This paper compares LSTM dengue forecasting models and demonstrates improved outbreak detection after incorporating mobility-adjusted imported cases and climate data.
This paper proves general global-stability results for a broad class of compartmental epidemic models with uniform susceptibility.
This study compares logistic prediction models with and without SMOTE for postoperative device infection, validating improved prediction in an imbalanced clinical dataset.
The study develops a photodynamic treatment product without a transferable epidemiological warning method.
This ecological analysis uses distributed-lag and additive models to quantify temperature-humidity effects and construct early warnings for intestinal infections.
The study combines cohort evidence with counterfactual simulations of HIV cluster transmission and resistance.
This practice-oriented article explains in-hospital early-warning scores, escalation triggers, digital integration, and their role in detecting infection-related deterioration.
This preprint is the same Petri-net reproduction-number framework and validation study as the subsequently published retained record.
This paper develops, simulates, and applies computationally efficient Bayesian spatiotemporal outbreak models with posterior outbreak probabilities and model comparison.
This randomized-trial analysis uses segmented change-point methods to identify doxycycline exposure thresholds associated with resistome and microbiome changes.
This review synthesizes artificial-intelligence applications, advantages, and implementation challenges for infectious-disease surveillance and early warning.
This scoping review compares wastewater-surveillance evidence for mpox early warning, including correlations, lead times, and diagnostic limitations.
This study monitors nutrient stress as an early-warning signal for harmful algal blooms rather than an infectious-disease process with a concrete transfer route.
This multi-patch mobility model estimates regional effective reproduction numbers and quantifies local versus mobility-induced transmission to evaluate restrictions.
This One Health review assesses China's surveillance architecture for zoonotic spillover and proposes directions for integrated early warning.
This theoretical work addresses ecological critical transitions generally and does not establish a concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
This integrated Bayesian ecological model forecasts avian salmonellosis outbreaks from climate-driven cone production and bird-irruption processes.
This application evaluates deep-learning forecasts of avian-influenza outbreaks using integrated web, environmental, and historical surveillance data.
This study compares Bayesian structural time-series and SARIMA-family forecasts and shows added value of search-index data for hepatitis B monitoring.
This narrative review compares dengue forecasting and warning-system methods, inputs, lead times, performance, and implementation limitations.
The study defines vaccination causal estimands and derives an unbiased estimator for directly averted outcomes.
This paper presents an open-source mpox genomic-analysis workflow for clade-aware alignment, mutation reconstruction, phylogeny, and outbreak classification.
This rapid scoping review and SWOT analysis evaluates an animal-health surveillance network against early-warning-system components and proposes improvements.
This study validates long-read genomic neighbor typing against reference distances, trees, and clusters for rapid Escherichia coli outbreak detection.
This preliminary wildlife application combines spatial heat maps, environmental data, and simulated reproduction-number transmission to identify canine distemper risk zones.
This review concerns immune-checkpoint-inhibitor pneumonitis, a treatment toxicity rather than an infectious disease.
This longitudinal wastewater study uses RT-qPCR and cross-correlation to evaluate lead times for norovirus and rotavirus clinical peaks.
This retrospective study develops, compares, validates, and explains stratified machine-learning models for anti-tuberculosis drug-induced liver injury.
This forecasting sprint compares multiple dengue models across states and develops ensemble forecasts adopted for public-health planning.
The study develops and prospectively evaluates automated outbreak detection and individual risk assessment.
This paper develops an ensemble post-hoc labeling method for heterogeneous infectious-disease time series and evaluates downstream outbreak classifiers.
This nationwide study models lagged associations between citizen-science tick reports and Lyme borreliosis cases to create an early-warning approach.
This paper develops a behaviour- and surveillance-adjusted framework for time-varying reproduction-number estimation and evaluates short-term forecasting calibration.
This systems review documents the evolution, limitations, and recommended early-warning improvements of China's foodborne-disease surveillance.
This prospective outbreak investigation combines clinical epidemiology, targeted PCR, and metagenomic sequencing to resolve an undiagnosed febrile outbreak.
This CHNRI consensus exercise systematically prioritizes global-health data-science and AI research, including infectious-disease preparedness applications.
This paper develops country-specific thresholds from travel-associated dengue surveillance to identify elevated risk where local reporting is incomplete.
This study fits and validates spatiotemporal GAMMs of vector abundance and weather to forecast malaria-risk proxies before outbreak activity.
This integrative review compares prehospital triage scores and early-assessment strategies for clinical deterioration among COVID-19 patients.
This retrospective time-series application evaluates routine mortality data and exponential smoothing as an early-warning system for respiratory outbreaks.
This application couples MaxEnt spatial risk mapping with SARIMA temporal warning to target human brucellosis prevention by risk area.
This preprint has the same title, authors, abstract, and machine-learning outbreak-detection study as the retained conference-proceedings record.
The study estimates generation time and basic reproduction number from contact-transmission experiments.
This comment has no supplied abstract or substantive methodological content beyond its title.
The study integrates multisource surveillance, phylogenetics, and epidemiological modeling of yellow-fever spillover.
This retrospective cohort investigation identifies excessive calcium propionate as the likely cause of a non-infectious chemical foodborne illness outbreak.
This paper develops and compares calendar-aware smoothing and bootstrap inference for Rt-based outbreak detection using observed and simulated COVID-19 data.
This study evaluates hospital, school-absence, serial, and parallel monitoring strategies against reported school infectious-disease outbreaks.
This prospective study compares NEWS2 and qSOFA discrimination for ICU triage, intervention, and mortality among acute pyelonephritis patients.
The study develops a multiscale model coupling within-host viral dynamics with contact-network transmission.
This study integrates explainable machine learning and Bayesian spatiotemporal models to generate district-level dengue predictions and warnings.
This regional surveillance assessment quantifies measles and rubella outbreak detection, response, immunization, and molecular-data integration gaps.
This study develops and externally tests a dual-branch deep-learning framework for wastewater- and environment-informed COVID-19 forecasting and tiered alerts.
This change-point evaluation concerns agricultural pest arrivals rather than infectious diseases and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This case study uses hospital wastewater detection, culture, whole-genome analysis, and multisource traceback for early cholera situational awareness.
This study compares tree-based models and develops uncertainty-aware quantile-regression-forest early warnings from integrated Salmonella food and health surveillance.
This simulation study demonstrates reproduction-number underestimation from misspecified generation intervals in phylodynamic inference and explains the mechanism.
This paper develops and pilots an open-source hybrid surveillance stack integrating heterogeneous signals, transmission models, anomaly detection, and calibrated alerts.
This evaluation uses localized clinical data, correlation, and change-point analysis to show wastewater tracked but did not lead Omicron cases.
This compartmental modelling study introduces a colonization amplification index alongside reproduction numbers to assess hospital and community Clostridioides difficile dynamics.
This general time-series omics software is illustrated with non-infectious developmental and plant data and lacks a concrete infectious-disease application.
This multicentre study develops, chronologically validates, calibrates, and interprets machine-learning prediction of carbapenem resistance from other susceptibility profiles.
This paper develops zero-inflated hidden-Markov negative-binomial outbreak detection for sparse Rift Valley fever incidence and benchmarks it in simulations.
The study develops and internally validates a multivariable nomogram predicting severe Epstein-Barr virus mononucleosis.
This mixed-methods implementation study evaluates adoption, completeness, bottlenecks, and outbreak performance of the 7-1-7 detection-notification-response metric.
This paper compares ARIMA and XGBoost using long-term national dengue data to forecast cases for digital early warning.
This qualitative study elicits clinician and researcher requirements, implementation barriers, and evaluation needs for acute-respiratory-infection early-warning systems.
The study develops a cross-modal temporal model for infectious-disease severity prediction and surveillance warning.
This scoping review maps maternal toxoplasmosis signs, risk factors, outcomes, and diagnostic strategies to inform early detection in African settings.
This comparative study evaluates MCMC and dynamic HMC for a Bayesian spatiotemporal hidden-Markov outbreak model using COVID-19 mortality surveillance.
This policy evaluation maps dengue warning thresholds against emergency declarations and models determinants of subnational response timing.
This outbreak analysis estimates serial interval and intervention-associated reduction in effective reproduction number for school-based pertussis.
The study integrates multispecies surveillance models to predict West Nile virus exposure risk one year ahead.
This interrupted forecasting analysis compares observed and counterfactual reporting after deployment of intelligent infectious-disease surveillance software.
This paper develops an iterated-filtering framework for changepoints in transmission parameters and applies it to seasonal influenza forecasting.
This operational evaluation presents a transfer-learning and anomaly-detection system that prospectively detects respiratory outbreak onsets and peaks across states.
This study compares reproduction-number estimation methods under weekend reporting effects using simulations and historical influenza data.
This wastewater study estimates adjusted lagged associations with reported COVID-19 cases and evaluates a 10-day early-warning lead.
This longitudinal wastewater study develops and validates a clinical-data-calibrated two-tier norovirus warning scheme with one-week trend forecasts.
This perspective synthesizes climate thresholds and recommends a locally adapted hybrid meteorological-trigger system for dengue anticipation.
This retrospective study evaluates NEWS2 discrimination, calibration, and threshold performance for mortality in very elderly emergency patients with infection.
This application combines spatiotemporal clustering, trends, and XGBoost risk prediction to expand rabies surveillance in laboratory-constrained Haiti.
This record describes travel-related cosmetic-procedure adverse outcomes but does not provide a transferable outbreak-detection or analytic design.
This preprint compares transmission-acceleration and endemic-channel thresholds for dengue outbreak detection across multiple geographical scales.
This implementation report documents a nationwide multi-scenario wastewater surveillance system and its early-warning role across several infectious threats.
This study combines wastewater surveillance and genomic analysis to show Salmonella Senftenberg detection preceded foodborne outbreaks by 7–14 days.
This paper develops and tests a rapid field-deployable DNA-extraction and fluorescence-LAMP workflow for Vibrio parahaemolyticus detection.
This workshop proceedings report summarizes leadership discussions and priorities without presenting a substantive research method, diagnostic, correction, or guidance framework.
This real-world pilot validates anomaly-detection warnings from primary-care encounters against local influenza-like-illness outbreaks and response actions.
This study evaluates whether a thermal-biology-informed reproduction-number model explains observed West Nile neuroinvasive-disease risk better than temperature alone.
This study develops a machine-learning framework using MALDI-TOF spectra and resistance profiles as rapid alternatives to whole-genome sequencing for hospital outbreak detection.
This study evaluates a cartridge-based field diagnostic for near-real-time influenza A environmental and wildlife surveillance at a high-risk interface.
This narrative review synthesizes outbreak-response lessons and prescribes institutional preparedness, surveillance, laboratory, and coordination improvements in Africa.
This mechanistic-statistical model addresses a forest fungal plant disease without a concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology in the intended library.
This review synthesizes RSV genomic evolution and epidemiology and recommends integrating genomic and multisource surveillance for early warning.
This nationwide study integrates climate risk assessment with model comparison and a random-forest warning threshold for infectious diarrhea.
This systematic review evaluates wastewater pathogen detection methods, early-warning value, and implementation challenges for gastrointestinal and respiratory surveillance in Africa.
This preparedness-oriented stochastic SEIRD ensemble and linked importation-hazard model projects Ebola outbreak size and cross-border risk.
This scoping review maps AI and computational approaches for genomic AMR outbreak-clone detection and identifies validation and implementation gaps.
This comparative study evaluates ways to incorporate patient-transfer histories into automated hospital outbreak-detection systems and quantifies alert trade-offs.
This paper establishes and simulation-validates a generalized next-generation-matrix method for calculating reproduction numbers directly from Petri-net epidemic models.
This commentary summarizes another review's findings without supplying substantive new methods or guidance content.
This nationwide blood-donor serosurvey identifies geographical evidence of undetected West Nile circulation and evaluates its sentinel early-warning value.
This mixed-methods evaluation assesses epidemiological attributes and stakeholder acceptability of mobile emergency surveillance in conflict-affected Darfur.
This study compares wavelet-SARMA and wavelet-SARMA-LSTM dengue forecasts and evaluates dynamic model-derived outbreak thresholds.
This calibrated individual-based model quantifies population impacts of school mitigation, testing, and quarantine strategies for SARS-CoV-2 transmission.
This Bayesian transmission-chain reconstruction estimates dengue generation times, spatial transmission, and intervention effects during autochthonous Italian outbreaks.
This systematic review extracts Nipah epidemiological parameters and models, pools estimates, and releases a reusable R data package.
This review develops criteria for assessing social-media data access and platform suitability for automated public-health threat early warning.
This perspective reframes Nipah early warning from reactive detection toward anticipatory One Health risk assessment and action-linked thresholds.
This modelling study fits age-structured measles transmission models to 172 serosurveys to estimate heterogeneous setting-specific reproduction numbers.
This title-only article offers no supplied abstract or methodological content sufficient to establish an infectious-disease methods contribution.
This study applies systematic review and meta-analysis while proposing standardized reporting for malaria immuno-epidemiology studies.
This analysis reports an increasing herpes-zoster incidence trend in veterans without presenting a transferable surveillance or analytical method beyond routine trend description.
This multicentre prospective network surveillance design estimates invasive fungal-infection incidence, timing, and burden among transplant recipients.
This title-only introductory editorial provides no research, guidance, diagnostic, correction, or reproducibility content for infectious-disease methods.

This review concerns low-grade inflammation and ageing rather than infectious-disease epidemiology with a concrete transferable route.
This prospective cohort assesses pneumococcal vaccination associations with cardiovascular outcomes rather than infectious-disease outcomes or a transferable infectious design.
This brief comment critiques bias and confounding in a reported XMRV association without providing substantive methodological development.
This national pandemic-influenza surveillance analysis estimates case-fatality ratios, risk-group mortality, and attributable fractions to target intervention programs.
This paper develops and simulation-evaluates model-based standardization of HIV trial results to a specified target population.
This national surveillance analysis identifies changing ethnicity-associated pregnancy listeriosis risk to guide targeted food-safety communication.
This paper describes a 12-step public-health triangulation method for synthesizing surveillance, behavioural, biological, and program data for HIV decisions.
This active population-based surveillance study measures changes in healthcare-associated invasive MRSA incidence across multiple metropolitan areas.
This title-only comment raises a generalizability concern but supplies no substantive method content for assessment.
